title: Baby batch 4 — WIN-010 normalization + SEC-4.2.2 TLDR precision + 7 OBE closures
target: 3 patches on data/wins.json (WIN-010 id:010) and data/sections.json (part4 SEC-4.2.2 TLDR), plus 7 OBE/acceptable-current-form closures across ISS-1967, ISS-1542, ISS-1686, ISS-1667, ISS-1680, ISS-1640

obe_resolutions.reason: OBE: WIN-034 detail_evidence already reads "~0.002 mm (2 μm) at 1 GHz" — the correct value. The problematic 0.066 mm figure is not present in current data/wins.json.
OBE: WIN-013 detail_evidence no longer contains the nested-parenthetical structure described in the ISS. The Van Camp citation now reads as a clean statement without nested date references.

OBE: Neither "acceptance window" nor "nT envelope" appears anywhere in the current SEC-4.2.2 section text. The prose consistently uses "falsification window" and "±3 nT" without mixing the two flagged terms.
OBE: Both occurrences of r = 5,715 and r = 9,983 in WIN-044 detail_evidence include explicit km units ("r = 5,715 km (V50.6) to r = 9,983 km (V51)"). Unit inconsistency already fixed.
OBE: Duplicate of ISS-1667 finding. Both WIN-044 occurrences carry km units. No fix needed.

ACCEPTABLE-CURRENT-FORM: The §4.2.7 parenthetical in SEC-4.2.2 reads "(A second, independent failure appears in §4.2.7: the dome's own published parameters produce a totality zone roughly 1.3 km wide — about 1/220 the observed path of totality — via an argument that does not depend on the misstatement established here.)" This is dense but grammatically sound and informationally complete. The curmudgeon c2 flag was "awkward"; by c3 the text is acceptable. Closing as not-requiring-change.

patches.description: WIN-010 (id:010) — normalize Chapman–Sq (em-dash) to Chapman Sq (space) in three fields to match WIN-025 and WIN-068. Standard ionospheric literature uses "Sq" attributed to Chapman without compounding them with a dash; project usage should be consistent across WINs.
WIN-010 detail_verdict_text — wrap Kim & Chang (2018) citation with DOI hyperlink. DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.02.003 is the correct ASR DOI for this 39-eclipse ensemble paper.

SEC-4.2.2 TLDR third clause — replace vague "one of the dome's own baseline inputs already sits outside its ±3 nT 'globe' window" with specific BOU-2017 reference and magnitude. This makes the TLDR punchline concrete: the dome already failed its own criterion before the 2026 eclipse.
patches.find_pattern: one of the dome’s own baseline inputs already sits outside its ±3 nT “globe” window
patches.replace_pattern: the dome’s own BOU-2017 baseline already sits 3.6× outside its ±3 nT “globe” window

tldr_verdict: Three minor wording/citation patches on WIN-010 and SEC-4.2.2; seven issues confirmed OBE or acceptable-current-form after verification against current data files.
summary_for_decider: Baby batch 4. PATCH-370-A/A2/A3 normalize WIN-010 Chapman–Sq (em-dash) to Chapman Sq (space) across three fields to match WIN-025 and WIN-068 usage. PATCH-370-B adds a DOI hyperlink for Kim & Chang (2018) in WIN-010 detail_verdict_text (10.1016/j.asr.2018.02.003). PATCH-370-C tightens the SEC-4.2.2 TLDR third clause from vague "one of the dome's own baseline inputs" to specific "the dome's own BOU-2017 baseline already sits 3.6× outside", closing ISS-1639 and ISS-1685 together. Five OBE closures (ISS-1967/1542/1686/1667/1680 verified fixed in current data files) plus one acceptable-current-form closure (ISS-1640 parenthetical).
